definition: Private credit NAVs are directionally overstated as Level 3 marks embed pre-2020 growth assumptions. Secondary market trading shows 5-12% discounts to NAV for 2024-2025 vintage funds. Healthcare staffing most exposed. Sector-specific magnitudes are speculative.
evidence: Level 3 marks embed pre-2020 growth assumptions
Secondary market trading shows 5-12% discounts to NAV for 2024-2025 vintage funds
DCF-based Level 3 marks embed pre-2020 growth assumptions
Healthcare staffing faces aging demand + labor scarcity = wage costs eroding margins
Sector magnitudes could be off by 5-10pp in either direction
open_questions: What is the true magnitude of overstatement by sector?
How do NAV discounts evolve as the maturity wall approaches?
Can mark-to-model practices be reformed before losses crystallize?
Which sectors have the largest NAV-to-reality gap?
How will mark-to-market adjustments flow through to fund performance?
Can automation in industrial sectors sustainably offset demographic headwinds?
